_comment: Template cron jobs for OpenClaw. Copy to ~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json and customize.

payload.message:
You are doing your nightly retrospective.

TASK: Review today's conversations and extract learnings from mistakes, corrections, pushbacks, or things that could have been done better.

STEPS:
1. Look for: mistakes, corrections from users, things that took multiple attempts, wrong assumptions, inefficient workflows
2. Classify each: coding / research / analysis / communication / operations / general
3. Write entry with format: ### [DATE] Title, What happened, Root cause, Principle, Applies to, Severity
4. Append to /workspace/learnings/{category}.md (Log section)
5. If HIGH severity, also add to Principles (NEVER COMPACT) section
6. Write daily summary to /workspace/learnings/daily/YYYY-MM-DD.md
7. Telegram output: brief summary under 100 words

RULES: Be honest. Don't fabricate. If clean day, say so.
Read /workspace/status.json and format a concise daily digest: 1) System Health, 2) Auth Status, 3) Sessions, 4) Cron Jobs, 5) Errors. Use bold headers, bullet points. Under 300 words.

Run weekly log compaction. Read all deep memory log files and learnings/*.md files. For any Log section exceeding 3000 words: extract principles to Principles section, summarize older entries, archive raw version. NEVER compact Principles sections. Report what was compacted.

Run weekly protocol promotion. Follow /workspace/protocols/weekly-protocol-promotion.md exactly. Read all learnings from this week, filter for promotion candidates (HIGH severity, recurring, applies-to-everyone), check against existing protocol rules, draft and append new rules. Report: learnings reviewed, promotions made, rule IDs. Under 150 words.
Read /workspace/status.json. Check auth section. For each profile: report provider, type, status, error count, expiry. Warn if any profile has errors or expires within 72 hours.
